{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Cloud Network and Infrastructure Engineer",
  "description": "Renmoney - We are a passionate team determined to challenge the status quo and make financial inclusion count for the millions of under-banked individuals and small business owners in Nigeria. We provide loans, savings, and fixed deposit solutions to our customers. Our vision is to be the most convenient lending company, delivering outstanding service experiences.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Cloud Network and Infrastructure Engineer

Job Location: Lagos

The Role&nbsp;


	The Cloud Network and Infrastructure Engineer is responsible for designing, implementing, securing, and optimizing the Bank&#39;s hybrid infrastructure across Amazon Web Services (AWS) and on-premises environments.
	The role ensures the availability, reliability, performance, scalability, and security of enterprise infrastructure while supporting business-critical systems, cloud adoption, disaster recovery, and operational excellence.&nbsp;
	This role is ideal for someone who has strong expertise in cloud infrastructure, enterprise networking, systems administration, and infrastructure security, with the ability to manage hybrid environments in a fast-paced financial services organization.


What You Will Do&nbsp;


	Design, deploy, administer, and support AWS cloud infrastructure, including compute, networking, storage, identity, monitoring, backup, and security services.&nbsp;
	Manage on-premises infrastructure, including Windows and Linux servers, virtualization platforms, storage, firewalls, switches, wireless infrastructure, and data center environments.&nbsp;
	Configure and maintain secure hybrid connectivity between AWS and on-premises environments using VPN, Direct Connect, routing, DNS, load balancing, and network segmentation.&nbsp;
	Monitor infrastructure performance, availability, capacity, and security using appropriate monitoring and alerting tools.&nbsp;
	Support business-critical applications through proactive incident management, troubleshooting, and root cause analysis.&nbsp;
	Implement and maintain infrastructure security controls, including IAM, access management, patch management, vulnerability remediation, encryption, logging, and firewall administration.&nbsp;
	Develop and maintain backup, disaster recovery, high availability, and business continuity solutions.&nbsp;
	Automate infrastructure provisioning and operational tasks using scripting and Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) tools.&nbsp;
	Maintain infrastructure documentation, architecture diagrams, asset inventories, configuration baselines, and operational procedures.&nbsp;
	Participate in change management, incident management, problem management, audit activities, and compliance remediation.&nbsp;
	Optimize infrastructure and cloud costs through resource utilization reviews and capacity planning.&nbsp;
	Collaborate with Security, Applications, Database, Service Desk, and Business teams to deliver reliable infrastructure services.&nbsp;


Requirements
Educational Qualification:


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or Higher National Diploma in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or a related discipline.&nbsp;


Professional Certifications:


	AWS Certified Solutions Architect (Associate or Professional).&nbsp;
	AWS Certified SysOps Administrator.&nbsp;
	CCNA or CCNP.&nbsp;
	CompTIA Network+ or Security+.&nbsp;
	Microsoft Certified (Azure Administrator), VMware, Linux, or equivalent certifications.&nbsp;
	ITIL Foundation certification is an added advantage.&nbsp;


Experience:


	Minimum of 3&ndash;5 years of experience in Cloud, Network, Systems, or Infrastructure Engineering roles.&nbsp;
	Hands-on experience managing AWS cloud infrastructure and enterprise on-premises environments.&nbsp;
	Experience supporting hybrid cloud infrastructure within financial services, fintech, banking, or other regulated industries is an added advantage.&nbsp;


Professional Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of AWS services, including EC2, VPC, IAM, S3, RDS, CloudWatch, CloudTrail, Route 53, ELB, Auto Scaling, AWS Backup, and Systems Manager.&nbsp;
	Strong networking knowledge covering TCP/IP, routing, switching, VLANs, VPNs, DNS, DHCP, NAT, firewalls, wireless networks, and network security.&nbsp;
	Experience administering Windows Server, Linux, Active Directory, virtualization platforms, storage, backup, and patch management.&nbsp;
	Knowledge of infrastructure automation using PowerShell, Bash, Python, Terraform, CloudFormation, or similar tools.&nbsp;
	Strong understanding of infrastructure security, access controls, vulnerability remediation, hardening, encryption, logging, and compliance.&nbsp;
	Experience with infrastructure monitoring, capacity planning, incident response, performance tuning, and root cause analysis.&nbsp;
	Excellent analytical, troubleshooting, communication, and stakeholder management skills.&nbsp;
	Strong ownership mindset with attention to detail and the ability to work effectively under pressure.&nbsp;
	Ability to collaborate across Infrastructure, Security, Applications, Audit, and Business teams while driving continuous improvement.&nbsp;


This Role Is Ideal For You If:&nbsp;


	You enjoy designing, managing, and optimizing cloud and hybrid infrastructure.&nbsp;
	You are passionate about cloud technologies, networking, and infrastructure security.&nbsp;
	You enjoy troubleshooting complex technical issues and improving system reliability.&nbsp;
	You thrive in highly available, mission-critical environments.&nbsp;
	You enjoy automating infrastructure and improving operational efficiency.&nbsp;
	You work well across cross-functional teams and can effectively manage multiple priorities.&nbsp;


You May Not Enjoy This Role If:&nbsp;


	You prefer purely application development over infrastructure and platform engineering.&nbsp;
	You are uncomfortable responding to production incidents or participating in on-call support.&nbsp;
	You dislike troubleshooting complex infrastructure and networking issues.&nbsp;
	You are not interested in security, compliance, or operational governance.&nbsp;
	You prefer highly repetitive work with limited opportunities for continuous improvement.&nbsp;
",

  "validThrough": "2026-08-16",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

Cloud Network and Infrastructure Engineer Job at Renmoney




        
    

        
        Renmoney - We are a passionate team determined to challenge the status quo and make financial inclusion count for the millions of under-banked individuals and small business owners in Nigeria. We provide loans, savings, and fixed deposit solutions to our customers. Our vision is to be the most convenient lending company, delivering outstanding service experiences.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Cloud Network and Infrastructure Engineer

Job Location: Lagos

The Role 
     

            
    




	The Cloud Network and Infrastructure Engineer is responsible for designing, implementing, securing, and optimizing the Bank's hybrid infrastructure across Amazon Web Services (AWS) and on-premises environments.
	The role ensures the availability, reliability, performance, scalability, and security of enterprise infrastructure while supporting business-critical systems, cloud adoption, disaster recovery, and operational excellence. 
	This role is ideal for someone who has strong expertise in cloud infrastructure, enterprise networking, systems administration, and infrastructure security, with the ability to manage hybrid environments in a fast-paced financial services organization.


What You Will Do 


	Design, deploy, administer, and support AWS cloud infrastructure, including compute, networking, storage, identity, monitoring, backup, and security services. 
	Manage on-premises infrastructure, including Windows and Linux servers, virtualization platforms, storage, firewalls, switches, wireless infrastructure, and data center environments. 
	Configure and maintain secure hybrid connectivity between AWS and on-premises environments using VPN, Direct Connect, routing, DNS, load balancing, and network segmentation. 
	Monitor infrastructure performance, availability, capacity, and security using appropriate monitoring and alerting tools. 
	Support business-critical applications through proactive incident management, troubleshooting, and root cause analysis. 
	Implement and maintain infrastructure security controls, including IAM, access management, patch management, vulnerability remediation, encryption, logging, and firewall administration. 
	Develop and maintain backup, disaster recovery, high availability, and business continuity solutions. 
	Automate infrastructure provisioning and operational tasks using scripting and Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) tools. 
	Maintain infrastructure documentation, architecture diagrams, asset inventories, configuration baselines, and operational procedures. 
	Participate in change management, incident management, problem management, audit activities, and compliance remediation. 
	Optimize infrastructure and cloud costs through resource utilization reviews and capacity planning. 
	Collaborate with Security, Applications, Database, Service Desk, and Business teams to deliver reliable infrastructure services. 


Requirements
Educational Qualification:


	Bachelor's Degree or Higher National Diploma in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or a related discipline. 


Professional Certifications:


	AWS Certified Solutions Architect (Associate or Professional). 
	AWS Certified SysOps Administrator. 
	CCNA or CCNP. 
	CompTIA Network+ or Security+. 
	Microsoft Certified (Azure Administrator), VMware, Linux, or equivalent certifications. 
	ITIL Foundation certification is an added advantage. 


Experience:


	Minimum of 3–5 years of experience in Cloud, Network, Systems, or Infrastructure Engineering roles. 
	Hands-on experience managing AWS cloud infrastructure and enterprise on-premises environments. 
	Experience supporting hybrid cloud infrastructure within financial services, fintech, banking, or other regulated industries is an added advantage. 


Professional Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of AWS services, including EC2, VPC, IAM, S3, RDS, CloudWatch, CloudTrail, Route 53, ELB, Auto Scaling, AWS Backup, and Systems Manager. 
	Strong networking knowledge covering TCP/IP, routing, switching, VLANs, VPNs, DNS, DHCP, NAT, firewalls, wireless networks, and network security. 
	Experience administering Windows Server, Linux, Active Directory, virtualization platforms, storage, backup, and patch management. 
	Knowledge of infrastructure automation using PowerShell, Bash, Python, Terraform, CloudFormation, or similar tools. 
	Strong understanding of infrastructure security, access controls, vulnerability remediation, hardening, encryption, logging, and compliance. 
	Experience with infrastructure monitoring, capacity planning, incident response, performance tuning, and root cause analysis. 
	Excellent analytical, troubleshooting, communication, and stakeholder management skills. 
	Strong ownership mindset with attention to detail and the ability to work effectively under pressure. 
	Ability to collaborate across Infrastructure, Security, Applications, Audit, and Business teams while driving continuous improvement. 


This Role Is Ideal For You If: 


	You enjoy designing, managing, and optimizing cloud and hybrid infrastructure. 
	You are passionate about cloud technologies, networking, and infrastructure security. 
	You enjoy troubleshooting complex technical issues and improving system reliability. 
	You thrive in highly available, mission-critical environments. 
	You enjoy automating infrastructure and improving operational efficiency. 
	You work well across cross-functional teams and can effectively manage multiple priorities. 


You May Not Enjoy This Role If: 


	You prefer purely application development over infrastructure and platform engineering. 
	You are uncomfortable responding to production incidents or participating in on-call support. 
	You dislike troubleshooting complex infrastructure and networking issues. 
	You are not interested in security, compliance, or operational governance. 
	You prefer highly repetitive work with limited opportunities for continuous improvement. 
    

    

        Click here to Apply

Logistic Assistant Job at Huko Advisory Services




        
    

        
        At Huko Advisory Services, we help businesses unlock their full potential through smart, strategic, and innovative human capital solutions. Based in Lagos, Nigeria, we provide HR outsourcing, recruitment, training, consulting, and workforce development services to businesses across industries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Logistic Assistant

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking a vigilant, disciplined and responsible Logistic Assistant to assist in all procurement, logistics and warehouse processes, structures and procedures.


Key Responsibilities
In addition to normal office routine duties (weekly/monthly reports), the person is supposed to be engaged specially to.


	Assist Senior?Log?Assistant for inventory and stock records.
	Assist Supply Chain Manager on Importation and Logistics duties.
	Reports to Senior Assistant.


Key Requirements


	Excellent working knowledge of Microsoft office suite.
	Excellent working knowledge of store administration.
	Excellent material know-how of Electrical and Mechanical items.
	Excellent working knowledge of tools and materials management on site.
	Excellent knowledge of inventory management.
	Knowledge of Book Keeping.


Skills & Competencies:


	Organizational skills.
	Ability to multitask and meet deadlines.
	Effective communication skills.
	Ability to work under pressure.
	Working knowledge of Microsoft Office suite.
	Work knowledge of ERP system.
	Knowledge of supply chain management.
	Work knowledge of Importation and clearing procedures.


Salary
Negotiable.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

  "title": "Senior Inventory Officer",
  "description": "DRYVA Logistics is an integrated logistics company, providing end to end supply chain management solutions. Our portfolio covers Third Party Logistics, E-Commerce Logistics and Warehousing. We are building a team of top talents who can grow fast into senior leadership.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Inventory Officer

Job Location: Rivers

Job Responsibilities
Inventory Controls &amp; Stock Integrity:


	Lead cycle counts and stock verification (bin-to-system and system-to-bin).&nbsp;
	Investigate variances (shortage/overage, wrong SKU, wrong location, damages) and track resolutions.&nbsp;
	Monitor segregation and controls for damaged/quarantine/hold stock and approvals for write-offs (where applicable).&nbsp;


Documentation &amp; Transaction Compliance:


	Verify completeness/accuracy of warehouse documents: GRNs, delivery notes, pick lists, dispatch notes, waybills, gate passes, seal logs.&nbsp;
	Ensure exception transactions have required approvals and are captured in an Exception Register.&nbsp;


Warehouse Storage, Controls &amp; Safety:


	Maximize space usage and safely put away items on the floor or racks&nbsp;
	Implement (FIFO, FEFO, LIFO) procedures for goods in the warehouses.&nbsp;
	Ensure good arrangement of storage facilities with appropriate layout, and signs display in strategic locations.&nbsp;
	Assign tasks and supervise the working of casual labourers.&nbsp;
	Ensure receiving and loading areas are top notch always.&nbsp;
	Ensure physical security of the Warehouse by restricting visitors and any other unauthorized persons.&nbsp;
	Implement and ensure clear safety procedures&nbsp;
	Maintain housekeeping standard at the loading docks and storage areas&nbsp;
	Conduct daily warehouse tour&nbsp;
	Ensure the warehouse is regularly fumigated against pest, rats, rodents etc.,&nbsp;
	Maintain and periodically service fire extinguishers.&nbsp;


Inbound Freight:&nbsp;


	Supervise the loading and offloading of goods into the warehouse by the warehouse loaders.&nbsp;
	Count all products, compare to waybill and check all shipments for damage, infestation and production code dates&nbsp;
	Report shortages, damages and mis-shipments on appropriate forms, and communicate any discrepancies observed during receipt or release of goods to the Warehouse Manager
	Witness Seal Cutting.
	Ensure timely dispatch of products from the DC with appropriate documentations and accurate update in close collaboration with Fleet Manager&nbsp;
	Ensure correct number and type of product is picked&nbsp;
	Compare quantity, labelling and address with order to ensure outgoing shipments are complete and correct&nbsp;
	Accept signatures for outbound as necessary
	Any other task that may be assigned.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their Resume and Cover Letter to: careers@dryva.com.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Senior Inventory Officer Job at DRYVA Logistics




        
    

        
        DRYVA Logistics is an integrated logistics company, providing end to end supply chain management solutions. Our portfolio covers Third Party Logistics, E-Commerce Logistics and Warehousing. We are building a team of top talents who can grow fast into senior leadership.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Inventory Officer

Job Location: Rivers

Job Responsibilities
Inventory Controls & Stock Integrity:
     

            
    




	Lead cycle counts and stock verification (bin-to-system and system-to-bin). 
	Investigate variances (shortage/overage, wrong SKU, wrong location, damages) and track resolutions. 
	Monitor segregation and controls for damaged/quarantine/hold stock and approvals for write-offs (where applicable). 


Documentation & Transaction Compliance:


	Verify completeness/accuracy of warehouse documents: GRNs, delivery notes, pick lists, dispatch notes, waybills, gate passes, seal logs. 
	Ensure exception transactions have required approvals and are captured in an Exception Register. 


Warehouse Storage, Controls & Safety:


	Maximize space usage and safely put away items on the floor or racks 
	Implement (FIFO, FEFO, LIFO) procedures for goods in the warehouses. 
	Ensure good arrangement of storage facilities with appropriate layout, and signs display in strategic locations. 
	Assign tasks and supervise the working of casual labourers. 
	Ensure receiving and loading areas are top notch always. 
	Ensure physical security of the Warehouse by restricting visitors and any other unauthorized persons. 
	Implement and ensure clear safety procedures 
	Maintain housekeeping standard at the loading docks and storage areas 
	Conduct daily warehouse tour 
	Ensure the warehouse is regularly fumigated against pest, rats, rodents etc., 
	Maintain and periodically service fire extinguishers. 


Inbound Freight: 


	Supervise the loading and offloading of goods into the warehouse by the warehouse loaders. 
	Count all products, compare to waybill and check all shipments for damage, infestation and production code dates 
	Report shortages, damages and mis-shipments on appropriate forms, and communicate any discrepancies observed during receipt or release of goods to the Warehouse Manager
	Witness Seal Cutting.
	Ensure timely dispatch of products from the DC with appropriate documentations and accurate update in close collaboration with Fleet Manager 
	Ensure correct number and type of product is picked 
	Compare quantity, labelling and address with order to ensure outgoing shipments are complete and correct 
	Accept signatures for outbound as necessary
	Any other task that may be assigned.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their Resume and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
